subject: Sports
title: Fast-Twitch vs Slow-Twitch Muscles
description: One-minute skill: Fast-Twitch vs Slow-Twitch Muscles.

hook.text: Sprinters and marathon runners use different muscle fibers. Your genetics decide which ones you have more of.

buildup.text: Slow-twitch fibers resist fatigue and power endurance activities. Fast-twitch fibers produce explosive speed.

discovery.text: Usain Bolt has roughly 80% fast-twitch fibers. Elite marathoners have over 80% slow-twitch fibers.

twist.text: You can train to improve either type, but the ratio is largely genetic. Champions are born, then made.

climax.text: This explains why no one excels at both sprinting and marathon running—the body can't optimize for both.

punchline.text: Your muscle fibers chose your sport before you did.

quiz.question: What type of muscle fibers do sprinters predominantly have?
